description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body and flat, circular ends. The exterior is composed of vertical wooden staves, each separated by narrow grooves, and the structure is reinforced by four dark metal hoops—two near the top and bottom edges and two around the midsection. The wood has a rich, reddish-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The metal bands are slightly weathered, suggesting durability and outdoor use. The top is fully closed, flush with the rim, and there are no visible handles or openings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A sturdy, reddish-brown wooden barrel with metal hoops and a closed top, featuring a classic bulging shape and rustic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, wood grain, and all four metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with similar features, highlighting the curvature and the seamless top surface.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the symmetry of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Another angled view, showing the consistent wood texture and the closed, flat top.
